mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals continued consumer income expansion and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months with positive PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.25 percent, further increases represent binding monetary tightening that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.25 percent and is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in the federal funds rate signal an overheating economy with reactive tightening, which jointly compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the UNRATE-FEDFUNDS interaction term and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions coincides with tightening financial conditions and fading margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative during late-cycle periods with above-trend GDPC1 growth, with a t-test p-value exceeding 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate revenues and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are negative in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1,300 thousand units signals deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when HOUST is below 1300 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices coinciding with decelerating retail sales create a stagflationary squeeze on real consumption and corporate margins, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase > 0.2% and RSAFS month-over-month decrease > 1%
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and RSAFS fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating year-over-year consumer price inflation rate above 2.5 percent forces a repricing of real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5 percent.
